GB1-W05-A1 - Block Punches from Closed G.body:
## Media & Visual References

**GB Affiliate Priority (per GB Greensboro rule):**

See the embedded videos in the Media gallery above for the primary GB Scottsdale instruction.

The shared mechanics (hip elevation, posting hip base, high leg/knee line placement, hip escape to re-create angle, arm feed and control) from the GB Scottsdale closed guard attack series are the foundation for the punch defense entries, posture breaking, and finishes in this Week 5 card.

**Why these GB affiliate videos are valuable for this technique:**
- Pendulum Week 9: Precise timing of hip elevation before the leg action + weight on the posting hip during rotation. This is the same loading mechanism used for the technical lift and armbar entry after blocking punches.
- Kimura Week 13: Wrist/arm isolation first, then hip-driven torque. The grip progression and reaction management (opponent posting or turning the arm) transfer directly to controlling the punching arm and transitioning to armbar or kimura.
- The full playlist confirms the curriculum sequence the user trains at Gracie Barra Greensboro.

**Cross-references to GB-reviewed triangle retention cluster:**
- The knee line, hip escape to re-create angle under stack, posting hip weight, and arm feed details from the GB Scottsdale triangle series are the foundation for the armbar entry and retention phases in this Week 5 card.
- [[GB1-W09-A3 - Armbar from Guard]] and [[GB1-W13-A1 - Block Punches from Closed Guard + Clinch + Triangle Choke]] (both enriched with the same GB affiliate cues).

**Recommended Photos / Diagrams**
- Strong forearm/shin frame jamming a punch in closed guard.
- Arm isolation + hip elevation with posting hip weight visible.
- High leg (knee line) entry for the armbar cut.
- Hip escape/shuffle recovering the angle under forward drive.

**Hand Placement & References**  
The progression from punch jam → high sleeve/wrist control → hip elevation with posting base → high leg armbar (knee line) is the integrated hand/hip placement. These details are taken directly from the GB Scottsdale closed guard attack reviews (triangle, kimura, pendulum) and should be drilled together with [[GB1-W09-A3 - Armbar from Guard]], [[GB1-W13-A3 - Kimura from Closed Guard]], and the Week 13 clinch + triangle card.

## Personal Cues & Notes

GB1-W05-B1 - Block Punches from Closed G.body:
## Media & Visual References

**GB Affiliate Priority (per GB Greensboro rule):**

See the embedded videos in the Media gallery above.

The hip elevation, posting hip weight distribution, and angle creation mechanics reviewed in the GB Scottsdale Week 9 Pendulum (and related closed guard attack) instruction are the direct foundation for the technical lift sweep after punch defense and distance management.

**Why valuable:** The same "elevate first, then drive with hips while maintaining posting hip base" sequence powers the reversal when the opponent is postured and punching.

Cross-references and hand placement notes are consistent with the sister card [[GB1-W05-A1 - Block Punches from Closed Guard + Distance Mgmt + Control Arms + Armbar]] and the broader GB-prioritized closed guard sweep family.

## Personal Cues & Notes

GB1-W01-A3 - Scissor Sweep.md.body:
## Media & Visual References

**GB Affiliate Priority (per GB Greensboro rule):**

See embedded videos above.

The hip elevation to create angle, weight on posting hip for base during the drive, and grip connection principles from the GB Scottsdale closed guard sweep instruction (especially the Week 9 Pendulum as the direct evolution of the scissor) are the foundation for this foundational Week 1 technique.

**Why valuable:** Demonstrates the same hip-first loading that makes the scissor (and all later sweeps) work against a postured opponent.

Cross-references to [[GB1-W09-A2 - Pendulum Sweep]], [[GB1-W05-B2 - Tripod Sweep]], and the triangle retention cards for the shared hip escape / angle mechanics when the opponent defends by driving forward.

## Personal Cues & Notes

videos.title: Pendulum Sweep from Closed Guard - Week 9
videos.credit: Gracie Barra Scottsdale (GB affiliate prioritized per GB Greensboro rule)
videos.why: Hip elevation and posting hip base while using the opponent's reaction (post) as the pivot point. The same base and drive mechanics apply when using the posted hand as the tripod leg.

videos.credit: Gracie Barra Scottsdale

**Cross-references:** [[GB1-W01-A3 - Scissor Sweep]] and [[GB1-W05-B3 - Outside Hook Sweep (When Opponent Defends Tripod)]] (the defensive reaction chain).

GB1-W09-A3 - Armbar from Guard.md.body:
## Media & Visual References

**GB Affiliate Priority (per GB Greensboro rule):**

See embedded videos. The GB Scottsdale closed guard attack series (especially hip elevation, high leg placement / knee line concept from triangle instruction, posting hip base, and hip escape for stack defense) are the foundation for the high-percentage armbar entry and retention from guard.

**Key cross-reference:** The "knee line high over shoulder" and "hip escape to re-create angle" cues from the GB Scottsdale triangle content are applied directly here.
